final_prompt.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: architectural photography, wide panoramic crop
Primary request: wide-angle low-angle shot of a karesansui (dry landscape) garden at a famous Zen Buddhist temple in Kyoto, Japan
Scene/backdrop: traditional Zen temple garden enclosed by low wooden verandas with evenly spaced pillars casting geometric shadows; aged earthen wall topped with moss in the background; autumn afternoon setting
Subject: raked white gravel with meticulously combed concentric circles and wave-ripple patterns symbolizing ocean and waves; several moss-covered rocks of varied forms distributed asymmetrically, representing islands and mountain peaks
Style/medium: architectural photography, 35mm film-inspired, photorealistic
Composition/framing: wide-angle lens (16–24mm), low-angle perspective emphasizing geometric beauty of raked patterns and spatial depth, horizontal panoramic framing
Lighting/mood: oblique late-afternoon autumn sunlight casting long elongated shadows from rocks and veranda pillars; warm, tranquil, deeply meditative Zen atmosphere
Color palette: warm off-white / cream gravel, deep moss green on rocks and wall cap, muted warm grey earthen wall, rich amber-brown aged wood pillars
Materials/textures: fine raked gravel with precise ridge-and-furrow texture, rough weathered rock surfaces with velvety moss, aged timber with visible grain, crumbling earthen plaster wall
Constraints: preserve the precise geometric rake patterns in the gravel as the dominant visual element; no people in frame; maintain the enclosed, contemplative spatial composition
Avoid: oversaturated colors, modern elements or signage, symmetrical composition, flat midday lighting
